steps.title: Set up the distance equation
steps.content:
For each part of the trip, distance equals rate times time. The first $x$ hours at 70 mph covers $70x$ miles. The remaining $y$ hours at 40 mph covers $40y$ miles. The total distance is 230 miles, so:
$$70x + 40y = 230$$

steps.title: Substitute x = 1 and solve for y
steps.content:
We know $x = 1$, so substitute into the equation:
$$70(1) + 40y = 230$$
$$70 + 40y = 230$$
Subtract 70 from both sides:
$$40y = 160$$
Divide both sides by 40:
$$y = 4$$
